explanation: Tests the multi-product weighting rule. Vercel Flags reached general availability — that's the flagship launch — yet the body buries it as a wrap-up sentence after four primary sections (AI SDK beta, Next.js canary, AI Gateway model access, platform behavior changes). The AI SDK section is a routine library bump that should consolidate, not lead. The Flags GA belongs as the first primary section. The fix is to reorder: lead with Flags GA, keep AI Gateway and Next.js canary as primary sections, consolidate AI SDK + Turborepo + platform retention changes into a wrap-up bullet group.

criteria.name: When the org has product-blog content alongside SDK / library content, blog leads
criteria.evidence: Vercel Flags GA — a flagship product launch — appears as a trailing wrap-up sentence after the AI SDK beta section, which is a routine library version bump.

criteria.name: Multi-product orgs must weight by user impact
criteria.evidence: AI SDK Voyage adapter (lesser) and Next.js canary improvements (lesser) occupy primary section weight equal to or greater than Flags GA (flagship).
